title: Malacothamnus howellii
intro: Malacothamnus howellii, also known as Howell's bush mallow, is a perennial shrub native to the coastal regions of central and southern California. It has been traditionally used by Native American communities for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall health and well-being. The bioactive constituents of Malacothamnus howellii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Malacothamnus howellii is commonly prepared as a tincture or decoction, and is often used in herbal remedies to support wound healing, immune function, and gastrointestinal health.
taxonomy: Malacothamnus howellii is classified in the following manner: it is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. This plant is further classified as a member of the genus Malacothamnus. The genus Malacothamnus itself is part of the tribe Malveae. Within the kingdom Plantae, it belongs to the phylum Angiospermophyta and the division Magnoliophyta. Malacothamnus howellii is also known as Howell's bush mallow.
